name: Nationwide Credit Clearing
description_short: Chicago-based credit repair firm with 25+ years disputing inaccurate credit report items. Offers 24/7 CreditProtect portal and free initial consultation.
description_long:
Nationwide Credit Clearing is a credit repair firm headquartered at 2336 N Damen Ave in Chicago's Bucktown neighborhood, with a secondary office in Phoenix, Arizona. The company has been in operation for over 25 years — placing its founding in the late 1990s or early 2000s — and serves both English- and Spanish-speaking clients nationally. No notable third-party certifications such as NFCC membership or HUD approval appear on their website, and their BBB accreditation status has not been publicly verified.

The company's core offering is fully managed credit dispute services. After enrollment, Nationwide Credit Clearing obtains the client's credit reports, identifies inaccurate, misleading, or unverifiable negative items, and handles the entire dispute process with the credit bureaus on the client's behalf. Clients receive ongoing monitoring with notifications when items change on their reports. The proprietary CreditProtect portal provides 24/7 online access to check dispute status without calling in. New clients receive a free credit report review and consultation, and enrollment can be completed entirely online. A referral program is also available for existing clients.

The company's most concrete performance claim is that it deleted over 25,000 items from clients' credit reports in the preceding year — a specific volume metric that implies a high-throughput, active operation. On Google, they hold a 4.6 out of 5 rating from 346 reviews, which is notably strong for a category that routinely generates consumer complaints. A separate Agent Login on their website points to a structured referral or affiliate network, likely serving mortgage brokers, auto dealers, and financial advisors who refer clients needing credit improvement before a major purchase.

For consumers with documented inaccuracies on their credit reports, Nationwide Credit Clearing's 25-year track record and strong review profile are genuine assets. However, meaningful transparency gaps exist: pricing is entirely absent from the website — no monthly fees, setup costs, or plan tiers are disclosed — making cost comparisons impossible without direct contact. No money-back guarantee is mentioned anywhere on the site. The company holds no publicly listed consumer-advocacy certifications, so consumers should approach this as a for-profit dispute service rather than a nonprofit counseling resource. Those facing debt overwhelm or needing structured budgeting guidance would be better served by an NFCC-certified nonprofit credit counselor.
pros: 25+ years in the credit repair industry — one of the longer operational track records in the space
Claims to have deleted over 25,000 items from client credit reports in the past year
4.6/5 Google rating from 346 reviews — well above average for the credit repair category
Free initial credit report review and consultation included at no cost
CreditProtect portal gives clients 24/7 real-time access to dispute status
Bilingual service in English and Spanish broadens accessibility
Two physical office locations (Chicago, IL and Phoenix, AZ) for clients who prefer in-person access
cons: Pricing entirely undisclosed on website — no monthly fees, setup costs, or plan tiers listed
No money-back guarantee mentioned anywhere on the site
No NFCC, HUD-approved, or other consumer-advocacy certifications listed
BBB rating and accreditation status unverified from available sources
No disclosure of whether disputes target all three bureaus or specific ones
best_for: Consumers with documented inaccurate or unverifiable items on their credit reports preparing to apply for a mortgage or auto loan
Spanish-speaking clients seeking bilingual, fully managed credit repair support
Individuals who want a hands-off dispute process with digital status tracking via an online portal
Chicago or Phoenix area residents who value the option of a local physical office
diagnosis: Nationwide Credit Clearing is best suited for consumers with verifiable inaccurate items on their credit reports who want a fully managed, experienced dispute service backed by 25+ years of operation and a strong Google review base. The central caveat is pricing opacity — the company discloses no costs on its website, so consumers must contact them directly before committing. Those needing debt counseling, settlement negotiations, or nonprofit guidance should look to an NFCC-certified organization instead.
services: Credit report acquisition on the client's behalf
Full credit dispute process management with credit bureaus
Identification of inaccurate, misleading, and unverifiable negative items
24/7 online account access via CreditProtect portal
Bilingual client support in English and Spanish
Refer-a-Friend referral program for existing clients
Agent/affiliate referral network with dedicated Agent Login
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
